regions.us-ports: Miami remains the lead port today with clear skies, west winds at 4 knots, and less than 10% rain probability supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Through the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds will increase to 8 knots under continued clear skies in Miami, while Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay will see similar light southerly winds and less than 10% rain probability, favoring uninterrupted cruise operations. On day two, Miami and the primary Florida homeports will experience overcast skies with southeast to south-southeast winds near 14 knots, but rain chances remain less than 10%, so no impacts to port activities are expected. Beyond 48 hours, overcast conditions with south-southeast winds around 11 knots will persist in Miami, Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise departures and arrivals. Other US ports remain free of operational weather concerns based on current forecasts.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, we have light southeast-southeast winds at 5 knots and clear skies across Nassau, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations with less than 10% rain probability in the Bahamas and Central Caribbean ports including Freeport, Bimini, and Key West. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to increase from the southeast at 8 to 12 knots with partly cloudy to overcast skies, maintaining less than 10% rain probability, which will keep port and shore excursion activities steady in Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Puerto Plata. By day three, southeast winds will strengthen to 15 knots with drizzle reported but still less than 10% rain probability, potentially causing minor visibility reductions and light dampness during tendering in La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná. No marine wind warnings are in effect, so sea conditions should remain favorable for cruise operations throughout the region. Passengers and crew should monitor for increasing cloud cover and light drizzle beyond 48 hours that may slightly affect outdoor activities but will not disrupt port operations.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds in San Juan are east-southeast at 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Day 2 brings east winds near 15 knots with drizzle and a 64% rain probability expected to impact port operations and shore excursions most notably in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix. By Day 3, east winds ease to 13 knots with drizzle continuing and a 47% rain probability that may affect shore excursions in St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Passengers and crew should prepare for reduced visibility and slick surfaces during these periods of drizzle, while day one remains favorable for outdoor activities across all ports.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing southeast winds at 7 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, resulting in smooth port operations and no impacts to shore excursions at this time. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southeast winds increasing to 12 knots with drizzle and a 22% rain probability, which may affect Costa Maya and Belize City shore excursions with light, intermittent moisture. By Day 3, a significant increase in drizzle with 55% rain probability and sustained southeast winds near 11 knots may affect port operations and shore excursions in Roatan and Belize City, requiring caution. Passengers and crew should monitor updates as the elevated rain chance on Day 3 could lead to localized delays in embarkation and disembarkation procedures in these ports. No marine wind warnings are currently in effect, and visibility remains good throughout the period.
regions.southern-caribbean: PRIORITY ALERT: Elevated winds sustained at 21 knots are forecast for Day 3, primarily impacting Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire. Today, Aruba experiences easterly winds at 16 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing for normal port operations and shore excursions with minimal disruption. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds steady at 19 knots from the east with overcast conditions and light drizzle in Aruba, while Curacao and Bonaire will see similar elevated easterly winds near 19 knots, which may require extra caution during embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, winds will increase to 21 knots from the east in Aruba with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, signaling continued elevated wind conditions that could affect docking and tender operations in Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, while Cartagena remains less affected with lighter winds and stable conditions.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, winds are ENE at 5 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, so port operations and shore excursions at Barbados and nearby ports like St. Lucia and Martinique are proceeding without weather-related interruptions. Over the next 24-48 hours, expect an increase to ENE 10-11 knots with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability on Day 1 and 12% rain probability on Day 2; Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten should prepare for these light drizzle conditions during embarkation and tendering operations. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift slightly east at 13 knots with overcast skies and 11% rain probability, which could bring continued low-visibility conditions in Dominica, St. Vincent, and Grenada, but no significant operational impacts are expected. All vessels should monitor evolving sea conditions due to increasing easterly winds but no Small Craft Advisories are in effect at this time.

regions.central-mediterranean: Today, northeast winds at 6 knots combined with a 40% rain probability are likely to bring scattered rain showers that may affect port operations and shore excursions in Naples and nearby ports including Civitavecchia and Livorno. regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds out of the south-southeast at 4 knots with a 25% rain probability and rain reported in Athens (Piraeus) mean port operations and shore excursions may experience minor delays or wet conditions, especially in Athens and nearby islands such as Santorini and Mykonos. regions.southeast-alaska: Today in Juneau, expect ENE winds at 8 knots with overcast skies and a 100% rain probability bringing steady drizzle that is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Ketchikan and Skagway will experience similar conditions with elevated rain chances and easterly winds around 8 to 10 knots, requiring adjustments for outdoor activities and vessel maneuvering. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a shift to east winds at 10 knots will bring 100% rain probability with snow in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway, which may cause delays or modifications to excursions and docking procedures. Beyond 48 hours, rain will continue at 100% probability with ESE winds at 10 knots, maintaining wet conditions through the region including Sitka and Haines, while Anchorage remains clear of significant precipitation but with steady winds from the east. Seattle and Vancouver report calm conditions with less than 10% rain probability today, supporting smooth embarkation operations.
